finding: `taskflow close --write` can land delivery and closure commits, set ledger `status: done`, and push planning commits, yet still exit `1` when planning-mirror residue remains classified in target `tasks status`. Operators interpret exit `1` as a failed close and retry closeout unnecessarily.
expectedBehavior: Close should distinguish hard failure from advisory residue: exit `0` with explicit residue warnings when ledger closure and governed commits succeeded, or return a dedicated residue status code with recovery commands instead of a generic failure exit.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed by adding `atm.taskflowCloseResidueAdvisory.v1` to successful close-write results when the remaining bucket is planning mirror drift (`planning-mirror-only`, `stale-import`, or `ledger-ahead-of-planning-mirror`). The warning includes the recovery command, now routed through `tasks import --write --reconcile-mirror` for done ledgers. Verification: `node --strip-types packages/cli/src/commands/taskflow/__tests__/close-residue-advisory.spec.ts`, `node --strip-types packages/cli/src/commands/tasks/__tests__/residue-diagnostics.test.ts`, and `npm run typecheck` pass.
followUp: Keep ambiguous residue fail-closed; only post-close planning mirror drift is downgraded to advisory after backend close and commit bundle success.
